question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
**Runtime Flow**
- The package entrypoint is [`index.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/index.js#L9), and it only re-exports [`lib/express.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/express.js#L27).
- `createApplication()` in [`lib/express.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/express.js#L36) returns a callable `app` function. It mixes in `EventEmitter` plus the application prototype, creates `app.request` and `app.response`, then calls `app.init()`.
- `app.init()` in [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L59) creates the per-app state and lazily constructs the router the first time `this.router` is accessed.
- `app.defaultConfiguration()` in [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L90) sets the defaults that shape runtime behavior: `x-powered-by`, `etag`, `env`, query parser, trust proxy, locals, mount path, and view settings.
- `app.handle()` in [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L152) is the request entrypoint. It builds the final callback, sets headers and circular refs, swaps request/response prototypes, ensures `res.locals`, then delegates to `this.router.handle(...)`.
- The actual request dispatch work after that is in the external `router` dependency from `package.json`; Express hands off to it here.
- `app.use()` in [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L190) is the main middleware/mount path. It flattens nested arrays, validates handlers, mounts sub-apps, restores `req`/`res` prototypes after child handling, and emits `mount`.
- `app.route()` and the generated verb methods in [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L256) build route stacks on the router. `app.get(path, ...)` is special because a single argument means setting lookup, not route registration. `app.all()` fans a handler across every HTTP verb.
- `app.listen()` in [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L598) is just a thin `http.createServer(this)` wrapper.

**Read Next**
- [`test/exports.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/exports.js#L7) to see the public surface area the package promises: `express.Router`, `express.application`, `express.request`, `express.response`, and the body parser/static helpers.
- [`test/app.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.js#L7) for app creation, callable-app behavior, `mountpath`, `path()`, env defaults, and 404 behavior.
- [`test/app.use.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.use.js#L8) for the most important middleware semantics: sub-app mounting, path stripping, arrays/nested arrays, and the `mount` event.
- [`test/app.route.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.route.js#L6) for route chaining, `next('route')`, and promise/error propagation.
- [`test/app.router.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.router.js#L11) for lower-level routing behavior: method dispatch, params, strict/case sensitivity, `next('router')`, and router-level errors.
- [`test/Router.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/Router.js#L9) for the router dependency behavior that Express relies on directly.
- [`test/app.listen.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.listen.js#L6) for the server wrapper and error callback behavior.
- [`lib/request.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/request.js) and [`lib/response.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/response.js) if your first change touches request/response properties, since `app.handle()` swaps those prototypes on every request.
- `test/acceptance/web-service.js`, `test/acceptance/error.js`, `test/acceptance/multi-router.js`, and `test/acceptance/mvc.js` if you want end-to-end examples after the unit tests.

If you want, I can do a second pass that traces one concrete path next, for example `express()` -> `app.use()` -> mounted sub-app dispatch, or `express()` -> `app.get()` -> route matching -> `next('route')`.
